SEE WORD MINUTES FOR DETAILS. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE CHAN. NOT REPORTED.
MINI-MINUTES FOR SEP-12-2008 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT PRODUCED UNDISPUTED FACTS SUFFICIENT TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE IN THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT ADDRESS THE FACTS EXISTING ON THE BUS PRIOR TO PLAINTIFF BOARDING. PLAINTIFF ALSO HAS PRESENTED TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT THAT ARE SUFFICIENT TO PLACE THE DRIVER ON NOTICE OF THE NEED TO PROTECT A PASSENGER AND THE HARM OCCURRED AS A DIRECT CONSEQUENCE OF A FAILURE TO DO SO. THE COURT RULED OF DEFENDANT'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS AS FOLLOWS: EXHIBIT C - SUSTAINED; OBJECTIONS 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 AND 7 ARE OVERRULED. ORDER ON OBJECTIONS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. MR. DOLAN TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER ON THE MOTION. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Deposition Questions, Psychological Evaluation, And Orthopedic Evaluation
Off Calendar Oct-29-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Scott D. Weiner, Deputy City Attorney, for for defendant and moving party: City and County of San Francisco
Francis J. Shehadeh, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Mark Principe

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to compel answers to deposition questions, psychological evaluation, and orthopedic evaluation granted:
1. Plaintiff Mark Principe and Tricia Principe's deposition shall resume within ten days of the date of hearing; plaintiff shall testify under oath in response to questions concerning his contacts with the third party witness Thatcher, and other matters not previously addressed in plaintiff's deposition;
2. Plaintiff shall submit to an examination by an orthopedic physician designated by defendant at a time and place to be designated by defendant, or otherwise by agreement of the parties;
3. Plaintiff shall submit to a psychological evaluation by a psychiatrist or psychologist designated by defendant at a time and place to be designated by defendant, or otherwise by agreement of the parties, and shall be limited to one full day.
Commissioner: Paul Slavit, Court Reporter: Tina M. Velasquez, CSR # 10072

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-16-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Scott D. Wiener of the Office of the City Attorney (554.4283) for defendant & moving party
Christopher Dolan (421.2800) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant has not produced undisputed facts sufficient to shift the burden of producing evidence in that defendant did not address the facts existing on the bus prior to plaintiff boarding. Plaintiff also has presented triable issue of fact that are sufficient to place the driver on notice of the need to protect a passenger and the harm occurred as a direct consequence of a failure to do so. The Court ruled of defendant's evidentiary objections as follows: exhibit C - sustained; objections 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 and 7 are overruled. Order on objections signed in open Court. Mr. Dolan to prepare a form of order on the motion. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
